title: T-118 — Sentry error-reporting SDK wired into apps/web + apps/worker (env-gated DSN, release tagging)
prompt:
# T-118 — Sentry error-reporting SDK wired into apps/web + apps/worker

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §5 Cross-cutting infra ("Observability: Sentry + Datadog / OpenTelemetry"), §13 Niefunkcjonalne (uptime ≥99.5%)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Wire `@sentry/nextjs` into `apps/web` and `@sentry/node` into `apps/worker` so unhandled errors are captured with release tagging + environment + tracing correlation to OTel (T-116). Today no error-reporting SDK is wired; auditor B Top-10 Gap #3 confirms.

This task delivers the **third leg** of the observability triple (OTel = T-116, pino = T-117, Sentry = T-118). DSN is env-gated; if unset, the SDK is initialised in no-op mode (warning logged once via T-117 logger). Release tag is sourced from `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` or `GIT_SHA` (CI sets it).

## Implementation contract
1. Add deps: `apps/web/package.json` adds `@sentry/nextjs`; `apps/worker/package.json` adds `@sentry/node`. Pin same major version across both.
2. `apps/web/sentry.client.config.ts`, `apps/web/sentry.server.config.ts`, `apps/web/sentry.edge.config.ts` (Next.js convention) — each calls `Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENV ?? process.env.NODE_ENV, release: process.env.SENTRY_RELEASE ?? process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA ?? process.env.GIT_SHA, tracesSampleRate: 0.1, beforeSend: redactBeforeSend })`. If `SENTRY_DSN` is undefined, log a single warning via `createLogger({name:'sentry'})` and skip init.
3. `redactBeforeSend` lives in a new `packages/observability/src/sentry.ts` and re-uses the redact allowlist from T-117 — strips `event.user`, `event.request.cookies`, `event.contexts.*.password`, etc.
4. `apps/worker/src/sentry.ts` (new) calls `Sentry.init` (same shape, runtime=node) and is imported as the FIRST line of `apps/worker/src/index.ts` (before `startNodeSdk`, before JobRegistry). Wrap each registered handler in `Sentry.captureException` rethrow via the JobRegistry's existing error handler.
5. Modify `next.config.mjs` to use `withSentryConfig({ silent: true, dryRun: !process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN })` — never upload source maps in CI without `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`.
6. Add `apps/web/__tests__/sentry-redact.test.ts` and `apps/worker/src/__tests__/sentry-init.test.ts`:
   - Mock `@sentry/node` and `@sentry/nextjs`'s `init` + `captureException`.
   - For web: trigger an unhandled error in a fake route, assert Sentry SDK `captureException` is called with the redacted event (no `password` field in event extra).
   - For worker: register a job that throws, run via `runOnceForTest`, assert `captureException` called.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/sentry.client.config.ts [create]
- apps/web/sentry.server.config.ts [create]
- apps/web/sentry.edge.config.ts [create]
- apps/web/next.config.mjs [modify — withSentryConfig wrapper]
- apps/web/package.json [modify — add @sentry/nextjs]
- apps/web/__tests__/sentry-redact.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/sentry.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/index.ts [modify — import sentry first]
- apps/worker/src/__tests__/sentry-init.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/package.json [modify — add @sentry/node]
- packages/observability/src/sentry.ts [create — shared redactBeforeSend]
- packages/observability/src/index.ts [modify — export redactBeforeSend]
- .env.example [modify — add SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_ENV, SENTRY_RELEASE, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given SENTRY_DSN set and unhandled error thrown in mocked Next.js Server Action, when test runs, then Sentry.captureException called exactly once and event object has NO password/token/cookie/authorization under any nested path.
2. Given SENTRY_DSN unset, when apps/worker boots, then single warning logged via @monopilot/observability and no Sentry transport initialised.
3. Given a job registered in worker throws, when runOnceForTest('failingJob') awaited, then Sentry.captureException called with error + job name in tags.
4. Given SENTRY_RELEASE env set to a SHA, when Sentry inits, then SDK release option matches SHA exactly (verified via mock init args); AND Given next.config.mjs loaded, when build runs without SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, then source-map upload skipped (dryRun:true) — no network call attempted.
## Test strategy
- RED: write the two test files with mocked SDKs before implementation. RED commands: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run __tests__/sentry-redact.test.ts && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test`.
- GREEN: implement config files, redactBeforeSend, worker init, env.example update.
- Run: above + `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not hardcode the DSN — env-only.
- Do not upload source maps in CI without `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — set `dryRun:!process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`.
- Do not send PII to Sentry — `redactBeforeSend` shares the T-117 allowlist; deep-walk the event before sending.
- Do not init Sentry twice in the same process — idempotent guard.
- Do not import `@sentry/node` from the browser bundle — only `sentry.client.config.ts` uses the browser SDK; server/edge configs are runtime-scoped.
- Do not block worker startup if Sentry transport fails to connect — `init` is non-blocking; queue retries.

pipeline_inputs.description: Wire @sentry/nextjs in apps/web (client + server + edge configs) and @sentry/node in apps/worker with env-gated DSN, release tagging, redactBeforeSend reusing T-117 allowlist, withSentryConfig wrapper. Tests assert capture + redact + release-tag round-trip.
pipeline_inputs.details:
SDK initialised before JobRegistry in worker; first line of process. DSN env-gated; missing DSN logs single warning. Release tag from SENTRY_RELEASE || VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA || GIT_SHA. tracesSampleRate=0.1 default. redactBeforeSend strips PII / secrets via deep-walk. withSentryConfig dryRun=true unless SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN set. Mocked tests assert capture is called with redacted event.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
acceptance_criteria: Given SENTRY_DSN set and unhandled error thrown in mocked Next.js Server Action, when test runs, then Sentry.captureException called exactly once and event object has NO password/token/cookie/authorization under any nested path.
Given SENTRY_DSN unset, when apps/worker boots, then single warning logged via @monopilot/observability and no Sentry transport initialised.
Given a job registered in worker throws, when runOnceForTest('failingJob') awaited, then Sentry.captureException called with error + job name in tags.

Given SENTRY_RELEASE env set to a SHA, when Sentry inits, then SDK release option matches SHA exactly (verified via mock init args); AND Given next.config.mjs loaded, when build runs without SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, then source-map upload skipped (dryRun:true) — no network call attempted.
test_strategy: RED: write sentry-redact.test.ts and sentry-init.test.ts with mocked SDKs before implementation; RED: pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run __tests__/sentry-redact.test.ts && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test.
GREEN: implement config files + redactBeforeSend + worker init + env.example update.
risk_red_lines: Do not upload source maps without SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN — dryRun:!SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN.
Do not send PII to Sentry — redactBeforeSend deep-walk + T-117 allowlist.
Do not init Sentry twice — idempotent guard.
Do not import @sentry/node from browser bundle.
Do not block worker startup if Sentry transport fails.
